answers.reason: Rotating imbalance in 3D requires inertia tensors, products of inertia, and 3D equations of motion. This is beyond the 2D planar rigid body scope.

solution_steps: Identify: 3D geometry (width × height × depth), rotation about an axis in 3D space.
Dynamic bearing reactions from imbalance require: F = m·e·ω² where e is eccentricity.
Centrifugal force magnitude: F = m·e·ω² = 10·0.005·31.4² = 49.3 N
But distributing this to bearings requires 3D moment equations with products of inertia.
This is out of scope. Tutor response: 'This is a 3D rotating dynamics problem involving inertia tensors. Our scope covers 2D planar problems only.'
